heading: Prohibited acts
label: 33114 Prohibited acts

L. 102–519106 Stat. 3396In subsection (a)(1), the words “which is manufactured on or after the date the standard under takes effect under this subchapter for such vehicle or major replacement part” are omitted as obsolete because the standard applies to passenger motor vehicles and major replacement parts starting with the 1987 model year. See 50 Fed. Reg. 43166 (1985).section 2022 of this titleIn subsection (a)(5)(A), the words “of any kind” are omitted as unnecessary because of the definition of “chop shop” in section 33101 of the revised title.

L. 102–519106 Stat. 3396In subsection (a)(1), the words “which is manufactured on or after the date the standard under takes effect under this subchapter for such vehicle or major replacement part” are omitted as obsolete because the standard applies to passenger motor vehicles and major replacement parts starting with the 1987 model year. See 50 Fed. Reg. 43166 (1985).section 2022 of this titleIn subsection (a)(5)(A), the words “of any kind” are omitted as unnecessary because of the definition of “chop shop” in section 33101 of the revised title.

children.text: manufacture for sale, sell, offer for sale, introduce or deliver for introduction in interstate commerce, or import into the United States, a motor vehicle or major replacement part subject to a standard prescribed under section 33102 or 33103 of this title, unless it conforms to the standard;
fail to comply with a regulation prescribed by the Secretary of Transportation or Attorney General under this chapter;

fail to keep specified records, refuse access to or copying of records, fail to make reports or provide items or information, or fail or refuse to allow entry or inspection, as required by this chapter;

fail to provide the certification required by , or provide a certification that the person knows, or in the exercise of reasonable care has reason to know, is false or misleading in a material respect; orsection 33108(c) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: own, operate, maintain, or control a chop shop;

children.label: B
children.text: conduct operations in a chop shop; or

children.label: C
children.text: transport a passenger motor vehicle or passenger motor vehicle part to or from a chop shop.

children.heading: .—Nonapplication
children.label: b .—Nonapplication
children.text: Subsection (a)(1) of this section does not apply to a person establishing that in the exercise of reasonable care the person did not have reason to know that the motor vehicle or major replacement part was not in conformity with the standard.
